title: eragrostis gymnorrhachis
intro: Eragrostis gymnorrhachis, also known as the finger grass, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical Africa. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Eragrostis gymnorrhachis include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, or extracts that can be used to make teas, tinctures, or creams for topical application.
